QF.id: XIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_65

question.text: Summarizing futures pricing across all three asset classes: if interest rates rise in India, what happens to each type of futures?

answer.text: Equity futures premium increases (higher carry cost), USDINR futures premium increases (wider rate differential via IRP), and IR futures/bond futures prices FALL (inverse price-yield relationship)
All three types of futures prices increase uniformly
All three types of futures prices decrease uniformly
Only bond futures are affected; equity and currency futures are immune to interest rate changes

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Rate hike impact: (1) Equity futures: F = S(1+r-d)t → higher r increases the futures premium (even if spot falls due to rate impact on equities), (2) USDINR futures: F = S(1+RINR)/(1+RUSD) → higher RINR widens the premium (INR at greater forward discount), (3) Bond/IR futures: higher yields → lower bond prices → lower futures prices. The DIRECTION of impact differs across markets. **The Trap:** The same event (rate hike) has different effects on different futures — this is the core insight of Series XIII cross-asset understanding. **Cross-Exam Context:** This question synthesizes Series VIII (equity carry), Series I (IRP), and Series IV (bond price-yield) into one cross-market analysis. Quintessential Series XIII content. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** RBI rate decisions create simultaneous trading opportunities across markets — understanding the differential impact is what separates multi-asset derivative traders from single-market specialists.
